q: Your organization runs machine learning inference at scale: 10M requests/second for real-time predictions. Model is 500MB. Latency SLA: P99 < 50ms. How to architecture endpoints across regions?
options: Single SageMaker endpoint in primary region

Multi-region SageMaker endpoints with auto-scaling, regional traffic routing via Route 53 latency policy, model caching via ElastiCache Redis (inference results cached), batch inference via SageMaker Batch Transform for non-real-time, cost optimization via Spot Instances for batch
explain: Multi-region endpoints enable local inference <50ms. Route 53 latency routing directs traffic. ElastiCache Redis caches results. Batch Transform for non-latency workloads. Spot Instances reduce batch costs.

q: Your organization runs 200+ legacy web applications (PHP, Java, .NET). Different deployment models (monolith, modular). Cloud migration requires minimal refactoring. How to modernize incrementally?
options: Elastic Beanstalk for language-specific runtimes (Java, PHP, .NET), CodePipeline for CI/CD, RDS for databases (Aurora for new, RDS for legacy), gradual refactoring to microservices via Lambda (high-ROI functions first), API Gateway facade for new features
explain: Beanstalk preserves language runtime familiarity. Gradual refactoring via Lambda (new features only). API facade enables progressive modernization. Minimal large-scale refactoring risk.

q: Your organization operates legacy billing system (COBOL, mainframe). Complex business logic, 40-year history of patches. Migration risk: high. How to mitigate?
options: AWS Mainframe Modernization program: automated COBOL-to-Java conversion, parallel system for dual validation (legacy + new), run 6 months in parallel, gradual traffic shift (5% per week to new system), rollback plan (if issues, revert to legacy), API compatibility layer for downstream systems
explain: Parallel validation reduces risk. Dual-run for 6 months proves equivalence. Gradual traffic shift. Rollback plan. Automated conversion. Minimizes business disruption.

q: Your organization implements automated cost avoidance: prevent expensive operations before they occur. Expensive operations: cross-region data transfer ($0.02/GB), DynamoDB on-demand at peak ($25K/month spike), multi-AZ (2x cost). How to prevent?
options: SCPs deny cross-region transfer for non-critical workloads, DynamoDB provisioned capacity with predictive scaling (prevents on-demand spikes), single-AZ for dev/test (except prod which requires HA), cost forecasting via CUR ML model predicts spikes, team empowered to optimize before crossing threshold
explain: SCP prevents cross-region transfer. DynamoDB provisioned capacity + predictive scaling prevents spikes. Forecasting enables proactive optimization. Cost avoidance > cost reduction.

q: Your organization is cost-aware SaaS: per-customer COGS goal $5. Current: $8. How to optimize to meet margin targets?
options: Right-sizing per customer tier (starter: shared RDS, professional: dedicated RDS, enterprise: aurora), auto-scaling per tier, compute optimization via Graviton instances (20% cheaper), storage optimization via Intelligent-Tiering, per-customer cost tracking enables customer-specific pricing
explain: Tiered deployment architecture. Graviton reduces compute cost 20%. Intelligent-Tiering reduces storage. Auto-scaling eliminates idle. Per-customer tracking enables precision pricing.

q: A 180-account org needs multi-account incident response. Incident in account A should isolate account, preserve logs, alert incident team, notify other accounts. How to automate?
options: EventBridge aggregator detects GuardDuty findings, Lambda applies quarantine SCP to source account, SNS alerts incident team across accounts, CloudTrail/Macie logs quarantine event, automated rollback after 24hrs if cleared
explain: EventBridge + Lambda quarantine + SNS notifications. CloudTrail audit. 24hr rollback. Automated incident containment.

q: Your 200-account org with 10K+ resources needs compliance attestation monthly. Proof that X% resources meet standard Y. How to automate evidence collection?
options: Security Hub standards compliance dashboard per account (aggregator), Config conformance packs prove evidence, scheduled monthly export to S3 (PDF report), third-party auditor API integration for automated evidence submission
explain: Security Hub dashboard. Config packs provide evidence. Scheduled export. Third-party audit API. Monthly attestation automated.

q: A 150-account org implements cross-account backup/restore testing. Production RDS database must be restored to test account weekly, validated, then deleted. How to automate safely?
options: AWS Backup automated snapshots, Systems Manager automation weekly: cross-account restore to test account, Lambda data validation (row count, checksums), auto-delete test DB after validation, SNS alerts on failure, RTO/RPO verified weekly
explain: AWS Backup + Systems Manager automation. Lambda validation. Auto-cleanup. Weekly RTO/RPO verification automated.

q: Your 210-account org needs resource-level encryption key access audit: who accessed which encryption key when. AWS KMS CloudTrail logs this. How to alert on suspicious access patterns?
options: CloudTrail Lake for real-time KMS API queries, EventBridge rule on suspicious patterns (access outside business hours, unusual principals), Lambda investigation (list decrypt operations per key), SNS alerts security team, quarterly key rotation audit
explain: CloudTrail Lake enables real-time KMS API analysis. EventBridge suspicious pattern detection. Lambda investigation. Automated security alerts.

q: A 190-account org enforces tagging compliance. Non-compliant resources (missing tags) automatically quarantined (no traffic). Teams must tag within 24hrs or resource deleted. How to implement safely?
options: Config rule detects untagged resources, Lambda tags with default values (infers from resource type + account), SNS notifies team, 24hr grace period to correct tags, auto-delete after grace period if still non-compliant
explain: Config detection + Lambda auto-tag + 24hr grace + SNS notification. Balances compliance enforcement with team flexibility.

q: Your 220-account org needs to prevent secrets in CloudFormation parameters. Developers must use Secrets Manager, not template parameters. How to enforce?
options: SCPs deny CloudFormation parameter creation with 'secret' or 'password' keywords, Lambda validates template before execution (deny if hard-coded secrets), Secrets Manager required via SCP (deny PassRole unless role has Secrets Manager permissions)
explain: SCP denies secret parameters. Lambda template validation. Secrets Manager mandate. Multi-layered enforcement prevents accidental leaks.

q: A 200-account org with SCPs needs to audit: which SCPs actually deny what? SCP conflicts causing resource creation failures. How to analyze SCP impact?
options: AWS Access Analyzer for unused permissions, Config rules test resource creation with different principals, EventBridge on CreateFailed events, Lambda analyzes SCP denial reason (DeniedByPolicy), quarterly SCP impact report
explain: Access Analyzer + Config rules test SCPs. EventBridge on failures. Lambda analyzes denial reasons. Quarterly impact reports prevent over-blocking.

q: Your 185-account org needs cross-region disaster recovery for critical systems. Failover must happen automatically within 5 minutes. Business has never tested failover. How to de-risk?
options: Monthly automated DR drill: EventBridge trigger → Route 53 failover to secondary region, validate application health (ALB target checks), automatic failback after 1hr, SNS alerts team, RTO < 5min verified monthly
explain: Monthly automated DR drill. Route 53 failover. Health checks validate. Auto-failback. RTO < 5min proven repeatedly.

q: A 240-account org with hybrid on-premises + AWS needs unified resource inventory. 100K+ resources across clouds. Inventory drift occurs (manual changes). How to detect drift automatically?
options: AWS Systems Manager OpsCenter aggregates AWS inventory, on-prem inventory via custom agents, drift detection via Lambda (compare current vs desired), Config compliance rules validate drift, auto-remediation for drift (terraform reapply)
explain: Systems Manager OpsCenter + custom agents. Lambda drift detection. Config compliance. Auto-remediation via Terraform. Unified hybrid inventory.

q: Your 195-account org needs per-department cost transparency. Departments see only own costs, can't see other departments' costs. How to implement secure cost visibility?
options: CUR to S3, Redshift with row-level security (RLS) per department (Cognito identity determines accessible rows), QuickSight with RLS (department can only see own rows), cost reports auto-generated per department via Lambda, scheduled delivery to department finance
explain: Redshift + RLS per department. QuickSight RLS. Lambda auto-report. Secure per-department cost visibility without manual distribution.

q: Your org needs federated machine learning: train models on-premises data without moving data to cloud. Data residency strict. How to architect ML pipeline?
options: SageMaker Federated Learning via AWS IoT Greengrass, on-prem compute trains local models, gradient updates (not data) sent to cloud, SageMaker aggregates gradients, converged model sent back on-prem, repeat for next iteration, data never leaves on-prem
explain: SageMaker Federated Learning via Greengrass. Gradients only (not data) exchanged. Data residency maintained. Model converges across on-prem + cloud.

q: Your org runs real-time bidding auctions (advertising): 100K bids/second, <50ms decision latency, bid amounts 0.1 to 100 cents (huge variance). How to optimize cost + latency?
options: DynamoDB for bid state (<10ms), Lambda for auction logic (provisioned concurrency), ElastiCache Redis for recent bid cache (<1ms), SQS for async winner processing, Kinesis for bid stream analytics
explain: DynamoDB + Redis < 50ms latency. Lambda provisioned concurrency. SQS async winner. 100K bids/sec at <50ms P99.

q: Your org operates social network: 500M users, real-time notifications (friend online, message received), feed generation. How to scale notification system?
options: Kinesis Data Streams for notification events, Lambda fan-out to user-specific SQS queues, DynamoDB for user notification state, API Gateway WebSocket for push notifications, ElastiCache Redis for feed cache
explain: Kinesis + Lambda fan-out. WebSocket for push. DynamoDB state. Redis feed cache. 500M users, real-time notifications.

q: Your org needs anomaly detection across 1M IoT sensors. Billions of data points daily. Detect anomalies in <1 second. How to architect?
options: Kinesis Data Analytics for real-time anomaly detection (statistical models), S3 for archive, Lambda for alerting, threshold-based + ML-based detection (isolation forest), SageMaker re-trains models weekly
explain: Kinesis Analytics real-time anomaly detection. Isolation forest ML model. SageMaker retraining. 1B data points/day at <1s latency.

q: Your org runs multiplayer game with dynamic in-game economy: player trading, item pricing, market clearing. Prices must be consistent across regions in near real-time. How to implement?
options: Global DynamoDB table (strong consistency per region, eventual cross-region), pricing Lambda triggered by trades, market maker Lambda for price adjustment, EventBridge for trade broadcasts, cross-region consistency <2sec
explain: DynamoDB Global Table for pricing. Lambda market maker. EventBridge broadcasts. <2s cross-region consistency. Multiplayer economy at scale.

q: Your org manages IoT fleet: 1M devices, firmware must update with zero downtime, rollback capability if new firmware breaks devices. How to architect OTA updates?
options: IoT Jobs for OTA (staged rollout: 10% → 50% → 100%), device heartbeat monitoring (if device stops reporting, rollback job), firmware signed (prevent unauthorized updates), metrics via CloudWatch (update success rate), rollback triggered by health check failure
explain: IoT Jobs staged rollout + heartbeat monitoring. Signed firmware. CloudWatch metrics. Auto-rollback on health check failure. 1M device OTA safe.

q: Your org builds e-commerce platform: 10M products, inventory real-time, customer orders immediately processed. Order processing must be ACID (all-or-nothing). How to handle inventory + payment atomicity?
options: DynamoDB transactions (ACID, up to 25 items per transaction), order transaction: check inventory, reserve quantity, charge payment, all-or-nothing. Failure → rollback all, SQS for async fulfillment
explain: DynamoDB transactions guarantee ACID. Order = inventory + payment atomic. SQS async fulfillment. 10M products, real-time inventory.

q: Your org needs fraud detection at checkout: 1M checkouts/hour, decision <100ms, false positive <0.5% (legitimate customers blocked). How to architect scoring?
options: Feature engineering: user history (DynamoDB), device fingerprinting, geolocation, payment method history, Lambda ML model combines signals (SageMaker), decision <100ms (provisioned concurrency), feedback loop updates model weekly via SageMaker
explain: DynamoDB user history. Feature engineering pipeline. SageMaker ML model. Lambda provisioned concurrency. 1M/hr at <100ms with <0.5% false positive.

q: Your org operates container orchestration: 10K containers across ECS + Kubernetes clusters. Need unified logging, monitoring, deployment. How to manage heterogeneous infrastructure?
options: CloudWatch Logs for centralized logging (ECS + EKS via Fluent Bit), X-Ray for distributed tracing, CloudFormation for ECS + Terraform for EKS (infrastructure as code), EventBridge for event-driven deployments across platforms
explain: CloudWatch Logs + X-Ray unified observability. CloudFormation + Terraform IaC. EventBridge orchestrates both platforms. 10K containers managed.

q: Your org needs to compress video on-demand: customers upload video, system generates thumbnails + poster frame, different resolutions (480p, 720p, 1080p, 4K). Process 10K videos/day. Cost-sensitive. How to scale?
options: S3 upload → Lambda fanout, smart routing: small files (Lambda Layers for ffmpeg), large files (MediaConvert), thumbnails via native Lambda, cost: $0.10 per video vs $1.00 MediaConvert
explain: Hybrid approach: Lambda for small files, MediaConvert for large. Cost optimization. 10K videos/day at $0.10/video efficient.

q: Your org runs data lake with open data access: 500 internal teams + 100 external partners. Data governance: sensitive data only accessible internally. How to enforce access controls?
options: Lake Formation with data classification (sensitive, internal, public), cross-account role-based access (external = partner account, internal = employee account), table-level and column-level permissions, audit via CloudTrail
explain: Lake Formation classification + role-based access. Column-level permissions. Cross-account roles for partners. Audit via CloudTrail. Data sharing at scale.

q: Your org needs global name resolution: internal DNS for 100K corporate resources (servers, databases, services). On-premises + AWS (hybrid). How to provide unified DNS?
options: Route 53 private hosted zone, on-premises DNS resolver (Route 53 Resolver endpoint) for hybrid, conditional forwarding rules, VPC endpoints for service discovery, CloudWatch for resolver query logs
explain: Route 53 private zones + Resolver endpoints. Conditional forwarding for hybrid. CloudWatch query logs. Unified DNS across 100K resources.

q: Your org manages APIs for partners: 50+ partners, each needs different rate limits, different API versions, different features. How to manage without creating 50 API Gateways?
options: Single API Gateway with usage plans per partner (rate limit per API key), API Versioning via header/path (v1, v2), stage variables per partner (feature flags), auto-generated API docs per partner
Single API Gateway, manage via auth context
explain: Single API Gateway + usage plans. API versioning. Stage variables for feature flags. Scales to 50+ partners without multiplication.

q: Your org runs recommendation engine: 10M users, 100K items. Cold-start problem (new users, new items). How to handle cold-start recommendations?
options: Hybrid model: collaborative filtering (warm users/items), content-based (new items), popularity (new users), SageMaker retraining daily on new data, A/B testing different models (control vs treatment), user feedback loop for model improvement
explain: Hybrid cold-start strategy. Collaborative + content-based + popularity. SageMaker daily retraining. A/B testing. User feedback loop. Cold-start solved.

q: Your org needs time-series forecasting: stock prices, demand prediction, weather patterns. 1B data points. Retrain models weekly. How to scale?
options: S3 data lake (Parquet), SageMaker Data Wrangler for preprocessing (parallel execution), built-in Forecast algorithm (DeepAR for 1B points), auto-retrain weekly via EventBridge, API for real-time inference via Lambda
explain: SageMaker Forecast handles 1B points. Data Wrangler parallel preprocessing. Weekly auto-retrain. Lambda inference API. Time-series at scale.

q: Your org has on-prem CI/CD pipeline (Jenkins, custom orchestration). Cloud migration: preserve pipeline, minimize changes. How to migrate?
options: AWS CodePipeline with CodeBuild, import Jenkins jobs via JCasc (Jenkins as Code), parallel to Jenkins for validation (dual-pipeline 2 weeks), gradual Jenkins → CodePipeline transition, artifact repository migration (S3 + CodeArtifact)
explain: CodePipeline + CodeBuild preserves CI/CD patterns. JCasc imports Jenkins config. Dual validation. Gradual transition. No pipeline interruption.

q: Your org runs on-prem service mesh (Istio, custom networking). Cloud migration to AWS: AWS App Mesh provides mesh-like features. How to minimize refactoring?
options: AWS App Mesh with sidecar injection (Envoy proxies), traffic policies (retry, timeout, circuit breaker), gradual migration per service, parallel testing (old + new mesh), canary deployments via traffic split
explain: App Mesh sidecar injection minimal code change. Traffic policies via config. Canary deployments with traffic split. Gradual service-by-service migration.

q: Your org has on-prem identity federation (ADFS). Cloud migration: use AWS SSO. How to maintain identity federation without breaking access?
options: AWS SSO with SAML federation to existing ADFS, parallel ADFS + SSO (gradual user migration 5% per week), application redirection to SSO post-migration, password resets via self-service portal
Switch all to AWS SSO immediately (risky)
explain: AWS SSO + SAML federation to ADFS. Parallel operation. Gradual user migration. Self-service portal. No access disruption.

q: Your org has on-prem secrets management (HashiCorp Vault). Cloud migration: AWS Secrets Manager provides vault-like capability. How to migrate secrets?
options: AWS Secrets Manager import, Vault agent on EC2 (on-prem) routes to AWS Secrets Manager via VPC endpoint, dual-read pattern (Vault + Secrets Manager), gradual application migration to Secrets Manager client
explain: Secrets Manager import. Vault agent bridge. Dual-read validation. Gradual application migration. No secret re-generation needed.

q: Your org runs NoSQL database (HBase on Hadoop). Cloud migration options: DynamoDB (managed) or HBase on EMR (self-managed). How to choose?
options: TCO: DynamoDB requires schema refactoring (query patterns), EMR HBase minimal refactoring but ops overhead. Choose DynamoDB if refactoring time < 3 months ROI (on-going ops savings). Gradual migration possible via dual-write pattern.
explain: TCO analysis: DynamoDB cheaper but requires refactoring. EMR preserves HBase compatibility. Dual-write enables gradual migration.

q: Your org has network appliances (Palo Alto, F5 firewalls). Cloud migration: AWS WAF, Network Firewall replace appliances. How to migrate without downtime?
options: AWS WAF + Network Firewall rules imported from appliances, parallel firewall (cloud + on-prem during transition), health checks on both, gradual traffic shift via Route 53 (5% per day to AWS firewall), retire appliances after validation
explain: Parallel firewall transition. Rule import from appliances. Health checks both. Gradual traffic shift. Zero downtime migration.

q: Your org has on-prem message queue (ActiveMQ, RabbitMQ). Cloud migration: Amazon MQ vs SNS/SQS (different API). Which to choose for minimal refactoring?
options: Amazon MQ for compatibility (ActiveMQ-compatible or RabbitMQ-compatible), dual-queue pattern (old + MQ during transition), application client library updates only (no code change), gradual message routing shift from on-prem to MQ
explain: Amazon MQ preserves protocol compatibility. Dual-queue transition. Client library updates only. Gradual routing shift. Minimal refactoring.

q: Your org operates on-prem log aggregation (Splunk, ELK). Cloud migration: CloudWatch Logs vs OpenSearch vs Splunk Cloud. Which minimizes refactoring?
options: CloudWatch Logs (agent-based, minimal config change), but if ELK dependency high, Amazon OpenSearch (ELK-compatible). Cost: CloudWatch cheaper for AWS services, OpenSearch better for multi-source logs, Splunk Cloud best for feature parity.
explain: CloudWatch Logs minimal refactoring. OpenSearch ELK-compatible. Splunk Cloud feature parity. Cost/refactoring tradeoff.

q: Your org has on-prem container registry (Docker Registry, Harbor). Cloud migration: ECR provides registry-compatible interface. How to migrate images?
options: ECR image replication from on-prem registry (manual push or AWS DataSync for large registry), lifecycle policies manage retention, cross-account ECR access via cross-account role, IAM-based access control replaces on-prem auth
explain: ECR replication from on-prem. Lifecycle policies. Cross-account roles. IAM access control. Registry migration with zero rebuilds.

q: Your org runs WebLogic on-prem (10K users, 50 GB app server instances). Cloud migration: AWS app server (Tomcat, JBoss) vs keeping WebLogic. How to choose?
options: WebLogic on EC2 (preserves existing setup, licensing portable), or WebLogic Kubernetes service (managed WebLogic), or Elastic Beanstalk for Java workloads. Choose based on: if Kubernetes familiar, use Kubernetes service; if not, use Beanstalk.
explain: WebLogic on EC2 preserves setup. Kubernetes service managed option. Beanstalk simplest. Choice based on ops expertise.

q: Your org has Oracle Forms on-prem. Cloud migration: Oracle Forms Cloud or refactor to modern web UI. How to minimize refactoring?
options: Oracle Forms on OCI with AWS-OCI interconnect, or Apex (Oracle's low-code platform) replacement for Forms, or keep Oracle Forms on-prem and migrate backend to AWS, Application Migration Service (MGN) for non-database workloads
explain: Oracle Forms on OCI (minimal refactoring). Apex replacement (low-code). Oracle Forms on-prem + AWS backend (hybrid). Depends on refactoring budget.

q: Your org runs SAP ERP + custom extensions. Cloud migration: SAP on AWS available via AWS Marketplace. How to ensure minimal disruption?
options: SAP on AWS via AWS Marketplace (appliance image), database migration via AWS DMS, custom extension validation in parallel environment, cutover during maintenance window (4-8hr), rollback procedure pre-tested
explain: SAP Marketplace appliance. DMS for database. Parallel validation. Maintenance window cutover. Pre-tested rollback. Minimal disruption.

q: Your org has niche on-prem database (Progress OpenEdge, Informix). Cloud migration: limited AWS support. How to handle?
options: AWS Database Migration Service supports some niche DBs (check compatibility), or EC2 self-managed instance (OpenEdge/Informix on Linux VM), or partner migration tools (Progress Cloud migration), or dual-run (on-prem + AWS) during transition
explain: Check DMS support. EC2 self-managed if DMS unavailable. Partner tools available. Dual-run for validation.

q: Your org scales rapidly: cost grows 100% annually, but revenue grows 150%. How to manage cost growth while protecting margins?
options: Unit economics tracking: cost per user/transaction, revenue per user/transaction, margin per customer. At 150% growth, cost can grow up to 150% and maintain margin (if revenue/cost ratio constant). Quarterly efficiency review ensures margins don't erode. Re-architecture per 50% scale milestone.
explain: Unit economics tracking. Cost per user drives optimization. Quarterly review. Milestone-based re-architecture. Margin maintained during 150% revenue growth.

q: Your org has multi-tenant SaaS: high-touch enterprise customers (custom features, dedicated infrastructure), standard customers (shared). How to allocate costs fairly?
options: Tiered cost allocation: standard customers (shared infrastructure, cost per seat), enterprise customers (dedicated resources + markup). Per-customer cost tracking via tags, margin calculation drives pricing, high-cost customers charged accordingly, low-cost customers subsidize standard tier
explain: Tiered allocation model. Cost tracking per customer. Margin-based pricing. Enterprise markup. Cross-subsidization for standard tier.

q: Your org implements FinOps program. Current waste: unused Reserved Instances, idle compute, over-provisioned databases. Estimated waste: 20% of $10M = $2M. How to prioritize optimization?
options: Optimization roadmap: Month 1 (RI optimization, marketplace sales, = $800K), Month 2 (compute right-sizing, spot instances, = $600K), Month 3 (database consolidation, = $400K), month 4 (storage tiering, = $200K), total: $2M savings over 4 months, resource allocation per month per team
explain: Phased optimization roadmap. RI first (easy). Compute next. DB consolidation. Storage tiering. $2M saved over 4 months.

q: Your org runs long-running batch jobs: overnight ETL, daily ML training, weekly reporting. Spot instances save 80% but interruption risk. How to balance cost vs reliability?
options: Tiered strategy: critical jobs (daily ML training) use on-demand (80% reliability), standard jobs (ETL) use Spot (20% failure rate acceptable with retry), cost: 50% savings overall. Checkpoint/resume for fault tolerance.
explain: Tiered Spot strategy. Critical jobs on-demand. Standard jobs Spot. Checkpoint/resume. 50% cost savings with high reliability.

q: Your org moves from CapEx (hardware procurement) to OpEx (cloud). CFO concerned: CapEx predictable (multi-year), cloud OpEx variable. How to provide budget predictability?
options: AWS commitment plans (Savings Plans, Reserved Instances) for baseline, on-demand for variable, forecasting model predicts total spend, committed + variable budget allocation
explain: Commitment plans for baseline. Forecasting for variable. Budget allocation baseline + buffer. Provides predictability while allowing growth.

q: Your org has NoSQL database (DynamoDB) with unpredictable traffic spikes (viral content, events). Peak: 10x average. On-demand mode auto-scales (cost-optimized). How to ensure cost doesn't explode during spikes?
options: On-demand with cost limits: DynamoDB auto-scales, cost anomaly detection alerts if exceeding 2x baseline, Lambda auto-remediation (cache aggressively, rate-limit users, queue excess traffic), max monthly cost cap via budget alert
explain: On-demand with guardrails. Cost anomaly detection. Lambda auto-remediation (cache, rate-limit). Cost cap. Handles spikes without bill shock.

q: Your org implements serverless-first strategy: Lambda, DynamoDB, S3. Cost model: per-execution + per-storage + per-data-transfer. How to optimize per-execution cost?
options: Right-size Lambda memory based on CPU need (faster execution = lower duration cost), batch operations (reduce invocation count), caching (ElastiCache Redis, CloudFront), CDN for data transfer (reduce egress cost)
explain: Right-sized Lambda memory. Batch operations. Caching. CDN. Optimizes duration + transfer cost per execution.

q: Your org uses Redshift for analytics: 40 nodes, $50K/month, utilization 40% (other times idle). How to reduce cost while maintaining query latency?
options: Redshift Spectrum (query S3 directly instead of cluster), materialized views (cache hot queries), scheduled cluster start/stop (scale down off-hours), cost: 20 nodes on-demand + spectrum for cold data = $25K/month (50% savings), latency maintained via materialized views
Right-size to 16 nodes (risk of exceeding query time SLA)
explain: Spectrum for cold data queries. Materialized views for hot queries. Scheduled scaling. 50% cost reduction, latency maintained.

q: Your org has cross-region data replication: high-bandwidth replication costs $200K/year. Data includes both hot (accessed daily) and cold (accessed monthly). How to optimize replication cost?
options: Selective replication: hot data replicated daily (cross-region), cold data replicated monthly (batch transfer via Snowball), on-demand restoration for rare cold data access, S3 transfer acceleration for cross-region, cost: $80K/year (60% savings)
explain: Selective replication frequency. Hot data daily. Cold data monthly. Snowball batch. On-demand restoration. $80K/year 60% savings.

q: Your org implements FinOps org structure: cloud finance team (2 engineers), but 500+ developers provision resources. How to scale FinOps without adding staff?
options: Self-service FinOps: cost allocation tags enforced by SCPs, cost dashboard self-service (team sees own costs), cost anomaly alerts to teams (not FinOps), automated remediation (Lambda fixes low-hanging fruit), FinOps team focuses on strategic optimization (cost models, pricing models)
One FinOps engineer reviews all requests (bottleneck)
explain: Self-service cost visibility. Automated remediation. FinOps team focuses on strategy. Scales to 500+ developers with 2 people.

q: A 210-account org implements automated account factory. New business unit requests account via ServiceNow, auto-creates AWS account + applies guardrails + provisions VPC + enables logging within 30 minutes. How to architect?
options: Manual account creation by AWS admin
ServiceNow integration via Lambda, Organizations CreateAccount API, Control Tower applies guardrails, CloudFormation StackSets provision VPC + logging, 30min SLA achieved via parallel provisioning
explain: ServiceNow → Lambda → Organizations API. Control Tower auto-applies. StackSets parallel. 30min SLA.

q: Your 190-account org implements automated security posture improvement. GuardDuty findings auto-remediated: excessive SSH access opens port 22, system closes it. But legitimate SSH access needed for maintenance. How to prevent breaking legitimate access?
options: GuardDuty finding triggers SQS (delay buffer), Lambda analyzes finding (is IP in approved list?), if approved, whitelist IP in security group before closing port 22, if unapproved, close port, SNS notifies team
explain: Delay buffer + IP whitelist check. Approved IPs exempted. Unapproved IPs blocked. SNS notification. Balances security + operations.

q: A 220-account org needs identity recertification monthly: management confirms employees still need their roles. Current: 10K+ roles, manual review 40 hours/month. How to automate?
options: IAM Access Analyzer + Identity Center, monthly audit of active role sessions (CloudTrail), email managers with report (who accessed what), require confirmation via link, auto-revoke if no confirmation after 2 weeks
explain: IAM Access Analyzer + CloudTrail analysis. Email managers. Link-based confirmation. Auto-revoke on silence. 40hr/month reduced to 5hr/month.

q: Your 200-account org manages AWS account lifecycle: creation, active use, scheduled decommission. How to enforce that abandoned accounts are decommissioned?
options: IAM policy: on day 90 of no API activity (CloudTrail analysis), tag account as abandoned, day 100: SNS alert owner, day 110: auto-decommission (backup resources to S3, delete accounts), exception process for legitimate inactive (data archive)
explain: Activity-based tagging. Progressive alerts. Auto-decommission after 110 days. Exception process. Accounts automatically lifecycle.

q: A 175-account org implements network architecture review: all VPCs + VPC peering + routing tables audited quarterly for security. 500+ resources. How to automate review?
options: Config conformance packs for network rules, VPC Flow Logs for connectivity analysis, Lambda generates architecture diagram (GraphML format), security team reviews diagram + flowlogs, CloudTrail logs all network changes, quarterly report auto-generated
explain: Config packs + VPC Flow Logs. Lambda generates diagrams. Auto-reporting. Quarterly review workflow. 500+ resources reviewed automatically.

q: Your 210-account org needs compliance certification: every 3 months, certify infrastructure meets standard X. Currently: manual attestation (error-prone, 100+ hours). How to automate?
options: AWS Audit Manager automates evidence collection (Config rules, CloudTrail logs, etc.), generates compliance report per standard, requires manager certification (electronic signature), tracks certifications per account/region, historical evidence retained 7 years
explain: AWS Audit Manager automates evidence. Electronic signatures. Historical retention. Compliance reports auto-generated. 100+ hours reduced to 5 hours.

q: A 185-account org with distributed teams needs to enforce resource location: product team in EU creates resource in us-east-1 (should be eu-west-1). How to prevent data residency violations?
options: SCPs deny all services in non-approved region per OU + team, Config rules detect out-of-region resources (daily check), Lambda auto-remediation (delete or move resource with alert), CloudTrail logs all violations
explain: SCP region restrictions. Config detection. Lambda remediation. CloudTrail audit. Data residency technically enforced.

q: Your 200-account org implements cost accountability: teams must stay within budget. How to enforce budget without breaking innovation?
options: Tiered enforcement: 80% alert, 90% approval required (manager can approve up to 110%), 100% block non-critical services (dev/test). Teams see budget vs spend real-time via QuickSight, monthly review adjusts budgets based on business growth
explain: Tiered alerts with approval gates. Real-time visibility. Monthly adjustments. Balances accountability + flexibility.

q: Your org runs multi-protocol API platform: 50K RPS, REST (60%), GraphQL (25%), gRPC (15%). Clients expect sub-100ms response. How to route protocols efficiently?
options: Network Load Balancer with TLS SNI-based routing, API Gateway for REST/GraphQL (JSON over HTTP/2), gRPC service on separate target group (HTTP/2 native), unified API Key authentication via Lambda@Edge
explain: NLB SNI routing. API Gateway for REST/GraphQL. gRPC native HTTP/2. Unified auth. 50K RPS multi-protocol.

q: Your org operates IoT platform for industrial sensors: 10M sensors, readings every 10 seconds, data includes location, temperature, pressure. Real-time alerts if temperature abnormal. How to process 100M events/second?
options: Kinesis Data Analytics for anomaly detection (statistical models), Timestream for time-series storage (optimized for sensor data), IoT Core for device management, Lambda for custom alerts, QuickSight for real-time dashboards
Kinesis Data Streams with simple processing
explain: Kinesis Analytics anomaly detection. Timestream for time-series. IoT Core management. 100M events/sec processed.

q: Your org runs graph database use case: social network (users, friendships, recommendations). 500M users, 5B friendships. Sub-second query latency. How to implement?
options: Amazon Neptune (managed graph database), indexes on frequently queried properties, caching via ElastiCache (warm queries), read replicas for read scaling, full-text search via OpenSearch for user search
explain: Amazon Neptune scales to 5B edges. Caching for warm queries. Read replicas. OpenSearch for search. Sub-second latency.

q: Your org needs complex event processing: detect fraud patterns in transactions (e.g., same card used in 2 countries in <1 hour). 1M events/second. Sub-100ms decision latency. How to detect patterns?
options: Kinesis Data Analytics for pattern matching, Kinesis windows (tumbling + sliding), Lambda for complex fraud rules (CEP), DynamoDB for transaction state (lookup same card recent locations), SNS for fraud alerts, estimated <100ms decision latency
explain: Kinesis Analytics pattern matching. Lambda CEP logic. DynamoDB state. 1M events/sec at <100ms.

q: Your org implements edge intelligence: 1000+ edge locations with local inference, cloud training, model updates. Edge devices have limited compute (CPU-only, 1GB RAM). How to run inference efficiently at edge?
options: SageMaker Neo compiles models for edge hardware (optimize for CPU), IoT Greengrass Core runs inference locally <100ms, cloud trains models weekly (SageMaker), IoT Jobs push updates (staged rollout), s3 model versioning
Send raw data to cloud for inference (high latency)
explain: SageMaker Neo CPU optimization. Greengrass local inference. Cloud training. IoT Jobs distribution. <100ms edge inference.

q: Your org operates on-prem Elasticsearch: 100 nodes, 5M documents, 10K queries/sec. Cloud migration: AWS OpenSearch provides Elasticsearch compatibility. How to migrate zero-downtime?
options: Opensearch Snapshot API for initial backup, continuous replication via Logstash (on-prem → AWS), dual-read pattern (query both old + new), traffic shift via client config (5% per day to AWS), retire on-prem cluster after validation
explain: Snapshot + Logstash continuous replication. Dual-read validation. Gradual traffic shift. Zero-downtime migration.

q: Your org has legacy monolithic Java application (2M lines of code, 200 engineers). Cloud migration: monolith on Beanstalk. Modernize to microservices incrementally via strangler fig. How to manage dependencies?
options: Strangler fig: API Gateway in front of monolith, new features as microservices, legacy dependencies handled by Facade API, synchronous calls stay monolith (replace later), async messaging for new features, gradual monolith retirement
explain: Strangler fig pattern. API Gateway facade. New features as microservices. Gradual retirement. Minimizes risk.

q: Your org runs on-prem Windows domain: 20K servers in 10 forests, multi-site replication. Cloud migration: AWS Directory Service (Managed AD). How to maintain domain consistency?
options: Single AWS Directory Service (limited to 1 forest)

AWS Directory Service with multi-site setup (AWS + on-prem), AD Connector for federation (on-prem LDAP trusts AWS AD), gradual DC promotion in AWS (replicas), site-to-site VPN for replication traffic, automated trust establishment between forests
explain: AWS Directory Service multi-site. AD Connector federation. Gradual DC migration. VPN replication. Domain parity.

q: Your org operates on-prem SFTP servers for customer file transfers. Cloud migration: AWS Transfer Family (managed SFTP). How to migrate without customer disruption?
options: Customers re-point to new SFTP endpoint (requires customer action)

AWS Transfer Family endpoint created, DNS CNAME points to new endpoint, dual-run (on-prem + AWS) for 2 weeks, customers gradually connect to new endpoint (monitoring old for staleness), on-prem server decommissioned after 2 weeks of zero traffic
explain: Parallel Transfer Family + on-prem. DNS CNAME update. Dual-run validation. Gradual customer migration. Customer-less disruption possible.

q: Your org runs 500 Lambda functions: mix of frequently called (1M/month) and rarely called (1K/month). Cost: $50K/month. How to optimize to $30K (40% savings)?
options: Provisioned concurrency only for high-volume functions (80/500 functions), standard concurrency for others, memory right-sizing per function (CloudWatch profiling), Lambda Layers for code reuse (reduce function size), cost: $30K achievable via selective provisioning + right-sizing
explain: Selective provisioned concurrency. Right-sizing. Lambda Layers. 40% savings via optimization.

q: Your org migrates from on-premises (capex hardware $10M/year) to AWS (opex $5M/year). Year 1 cost: hardware depreciation $2M + AWS $5M = $7M (net positive). But CFO budget set for $10M on-prem model. How to align budgets?
options: Phased migration: keep 50% on-prem (year 1: $5M hardware + $2.5M AWS = $7.5M, within budget), year 2: 25% on-prem ($2.5M + $3.75M = $6.25M), year 3: 0% on-prem ($5M AWS only). Total 3-year TCO same, but reduces year-over-year shock.
explain: Phased migration aligns with budget. Year 1 $7.5M. Year 2 $6.25M. Year 3 $5M. No budget overrun.

q: Your org implements cost optimization: every engineer paid bonus if team achieves cost target (e.g., $10/user/month). How to prevent engineers from cutting legitimate features to save costs?
options: Dual metrics: cost target + quality metrics (latency, availability, feature count). Bonus only if BOTH achieved. Cost incentive: up to 50% of savings. Quality guardrails: non-negotiable SLA + feature backlog tracking.
explain: Dual metrics prevent quality erosion. Cost + quality both required. Shared savings incentive. Balanced optimization.

q: Your org uses Reserved Instances: purchased 10,000 × m5.large (3-year contract). Business changes mid-contract: only 5,000 needed. Loss: $2M if holding idle. How to minimize loss?
options: Sell 5,000 RIs on RI Marketplace at 70% value (recover $1.4M), loss only $600K. Alternatively: right-size to m6i (smaller, cheaper), purchase new RIs for m6i (offset loss via cost savings). Net loss: <$300K via strategy.
explain: RI Marketplace recover 70%. Right-size strategy. Dual approach minimizes loss to <$300K.

q: Your 160-account org implements automated backup testing for all databases. Failed restore test alerts team immediately. How to prevent test failures from blocking production?
options: AWS Backup cross-account restore (test account isolated), Lambda validation (fast-fail), parallel test execution per database, CloudWatch metrics track test pass rate, failed tests → SNS alert but production unaffected
explain: Cross-account isolation. Parallel execution. CloudWatch metrics. Production unaffected by test failures.

q: A 170-account org implements automated security patch deployment: patch released, auto-apply to dev within 24hrs, test within 48hrs, prod within 72hrs. Patch failure → auto-rollback. How to implement safely?
options: AWS Patch Manager with maintenance windows (dev 24hr, test 48hr, prod 72hr), Systems Manager automation applies patches, CloudWatch alarms on patch failure, auto-rollback to previous AMI version, SNS alerts team on failure
explain: Patch Manager with tiered windows. Automation applies. Auto-rollback on failure. SNS alerts. Phased deployment reduces risk.

q: Your 200-account org needs to audit: which employees have AWS console access? Who accessed what? How to generate monthly report?

q: A 180-account org needs API rate limiting per customer (100 calls/hour for free, 10K/hour for premium). How to enforce without custom code?

q: Your 195-account org implements cost governance: each team budget $50K/month. Overspend detected: auto-remediation or escalation? How to balance innovation + cost control?

explain: Tiered thresholds with approval gates. Real-time visibility. Exception process. Balances control + flexibility.

q: A 210-account org needs to retire resources: RDS instance marked for deletion (end-of-support), must delete all snapshots + automated backups before deletion. How to enforce order?

q: Your org operates analytics platform: dashboard queries (1 sec), ad-hoc queries (1 min), background ML jobs (1 hr). Mixed workload. How to optimize warehouse cost?

Redshift for dashboard (provisioned), Athena for ad-hoc (on-demand pay-per-query), EMR for ML jobs (Spot instances), cost: dashboard $10K, ad-hoc $2K, ML $1K vs $25K single cluster
explain: Tiered architecture. Redshift dashboard. Athena ad-hoc. EMR ML. 85% cost reduction vs single cluster.

q: Your org implements chargeback to business units. IT costs $10M/year, distributed: compute $4M, storage $2M, network $2M, shared services $2M. How to allocate shared services fairly?

q: Your org needs distributed lock management for critical sections: 100K concurrent requests, mutual exclusion required, lock timeout 5 seconds. How to implement?

q: Your org has EXA database on-premises. Cloud migration: AWS does not have EXA equivalent. How to approach?

q: Your org runs multi-tenant SaaS with 1000 customers. Per-customer cost varies 10x (large customers use more resources). How to build multi-tier pricing model that customers accept?

explain: Usage-based pricing transparent. Per-customer cost tracking. Tiered markup. Cost calculator builds trust.

q: Your 180-account org needs to enforce security standards automatically. Non-compliant resources auto-tagged for remediation. How to prevent false positives?

explain: 2-person rule for detection. SNS alerts before action. Dry-run preview. Approval gate for destructive actions.

q: Your org implements disaster recovery for entire data center (100+ applications, heterogeneous infrastructure, RTO 4 hours). How to test DR without breaking production?
options: AWS Application Discovery Service maps dependencies, Replication Manager fails over to secondary region monthly (automated), CloudFormation validates environment (smoke tests), production traffic unaffected (secondary environment isolated), metrics validate RTO <4 hours
explain: Discovery Service + Replication Manager. Monthly automated failover test. Secondary environment isolated. RTO validated monthly.

q: Your org negotiates AWS pricing: $100M annual spend, request 25% discount. AWS offers 15%. How to bridge gap?
options: Negotiation strategy: commit 3-year (earns 25%), or commit $75M annually (earns 18%), or hybrid: $60M 3-year + $25M 1-year = earn 22% blended. Result: negotiate 3-year + 1-year combo to reach target discount with flexibility.
